items.title: Travel Guide
items.summary: Welcome to the conference! Most international participants will arrive at Soekarno-Hatta International Airport (CGK) in Jakarta. To reach the ITB campus in Bandung, please choose from the following transportation options: (source : kcic.co.id) Travel Time: ~45 minutes (train only) + 30 minutes transit. Departure: Halim Station, Jakarta. Arrival at ITB: Take the train to Padalarang Station. Switch to the Feeder Train (included in your ticket) to Bandung Station (City Center). From Bandung Station, ITB is a short 10–15 minute taxi/Grab ride away. Booking: Whoosh Official Website or the Whoosh App. (source: infobanknews.com) This is the most convenient option as it…
items.content_html:
Welcome to the conference! Most international participants will arrive at Soekarno-Hatta International Airport (CGK) in Jakarta. To reach the ITB campus in Bandung, please choose from the following transportation options:

1. Whoosh High-Speed Rail

(source : kcic.co.id)

  • Travel Time: ~45 minutes (train only) + 30 minutes transit.

  • Departure: Halim Station, Jakarta.

  • Arrival at ITB: Take the train to Padalarang Station.

    • Switch to the Feeder Train (included in your ticket) to Bandung Station (City Center).

    • From Bandung Station, ITB is a short 10–15 minute taxi/Grab ride away.

  • Booking: Whoosh Official Website or the Whoosh App.

2. Direct Airport Shuttle

(source: infobanknews.com)

This is the most convenient option as it avoids transferring luggage between trains and taxis.

  • Travel Time: 3–4 hours (depending on traffic).

  • Departure: Direct from Soekarno-Hatta Airport (all terminals).

  • Recommended Operators for ITB: City Trans, Jackal Holidays. These have drop-off points very close to the ITB area (Pasteur / Dipatiukur).

    • Primajasa, Xtrans, DayTrans: Reliable alternatives with various drop-off points.

  • Booking: Airport counters, Traveloka, or RedBus.

3. Regular Train (KAI)

(source: traveloka.com)

  • Travel Time: ~2 hours 50 minutes.

  • Departure: Gambir Station, Jakarta (Reach this via DAMRI bus or taxi from the airport).

  • Arrival: Bandung Station. From here, ITB is just 3km away.

  • Booking: KAI Official Website or KAI Access app.

Quick Tips for ITB Visitors:

  • Traffic Alert: Friday afternoons and holiday weekends can significantly increase travel time by road. If you are arriving during these times, the Whoosh or Regular Train is highly recommended to avoid highway congestion.

  • Local Transport: Once in Bandung, the easiest way to get to the ITB gate is via Grab or Gojek (download the apps in advance).

  • Station Choice: If taking the Whoosh, do not get off at Tegalluar; it is very far from ITB. Always choose Padalarang and use the Feeder Train to reach the city center.

ICoDSE 2026, the 12th edition of the conference, will focus on Generative AI seen from the lens of practitioners and the body of software and data engineering. The conference will explore how generative AI and other AI technology enhances secure systems development through intelligent code…
items.content_html:
2026 IEEE International Conference on Data and Software Engineering (ICoDSE) 

ICoDSE (International Conference on Data and Software Engineering) is an international conference focused on the latest developments in data and software engineering. It brings together researchers, practitioners, and professionals to share knowledge on topics such as big data processing, software development, artificial intelligence, and more.

ICoDSE 2026, the 12th edition of the conference, will focus on Generative AI seen from the lens of practitioners and the body of software and data engineering. The conference will explore how generative AI and other AI technology enhances secure systems development through intelligent code generation, intelligent test automation, and synthetic data creation. It will also emphasize the importance of solid data and software engineering foundations & clean data pipelines, scalable architectures, and robust deployment practices in supporting AI applications for secure systems. This symbiotic relationship forms a cycle where generative AI advances of software/data engineering, and vice versa.

Venue
Aula Barat, Institut Teknologi Bandung

Program Schedule

Coming Soon

items.title: Call for Papers

Organized by: Also co-organized by: Conference Theme: “Generative AI Meets Data and Software Engineering: Challenges and Innovations” This year’s edition focuses on the intersection between Generative AI and…
items.content_html:
CALL FOR PAPERS
2025 IEEE International Conference on Data and Software Engineering (ICoDSE)
📅 October 28–29, 2025 | 📍 Batam, Indonesia (Hybrid)
🌐 https://icodse.org | 📩 icodse@informatika.org

Dear Colleagues,

We are delighted to invite you to the 2025 IEEE International Conference on Data and Software Engineering (ICoDSE) — a prestigious, IEEE-indexed event that brings together global researchers, practitioners, and industry experts to discuss groundbreaking advances in software systems, data, and intelligent technologies.

Organized by:

  • School of Electrical Engineering and Informatics, Institut Teknologi Bandung (STEI ITB)
  • Politeknik Negeri Batam
  • Universiti Teknologi PETRONAS (UTP)

Also co-organized by:

  • Universitas Maritim Raja Ali Haji

Conference Theme:
“Generative AI Meets Data and Software Engineering: Challenges and Innovations”

This year’s edition focuses on the intersection between Generative AI and Software/Data Engineering, exploring how GenAI transforms development workflows and data pipelines, and how advancements in these fields drive the future of AI.

🎤 Keynote Speakers:

  • Assoc. Prof. Christoph Treude – SMU, Singapore
  • Univ. Prof. Marta Sabou, Ph.D. – WU, Austria
  • Assoc. Prof. Dr. Ir. G.A. Putri Saptawati, M.Comm. – ITB, Indonesia
  • Shrinath Thube – IBM Software Developer and IEEE Senior Member

Special Session by Dr. Ganesh Kumar: Generative AI for Education — Transforming Learning, Teaching, and Assessment
This special session will explore how Generative AI technologies are revolutionizing education from personalized learning and curriculum design to adaptive assessment, intelligent tutoring, and ethical considerations in AI-powered classrooms. Topics include AI assisted content creation, LLM based student support, learning analytics, educational chatbots, and ensuring data privacy and fairness in AI tools for education.

📆 Important Dates:

  • Paper Submission Deadline: Sep 5 September 19, 2025 extended!
  • Notification of Acceptance: Oct 4 October 11, 2025 extended!
  • Registration: October 4–21, 2025
  • Camera-Ready: October 21, 2025
  • Conference Dates: October 28–29, 2025

📄 Paper Submission:

  • Format: IEEE standard template (Word or LaTeX)
  • Submission Platform: To be announced on the website

All accepted and presented papers will be submitted to IEEE Xplore for publication and indexing.

💰 Registration Fee:

International Participants:

  • Presenter: USD 300
  • Presenter (IEEE Member): USD 250
  • Presenter (Student): USD 250
  • Presenter (Student – IEEE): USD 200
  • Non-Presenter / Attendee: USD 150

Domestic Participants:

  • Presenter: Rp 3.500.000,-
  • Presenter (IEEE Member): Rp 3.000.000,-
  • Presenter (Student): Rp 2.500.000,-
  • Presenter (Student – IEEE): Rp 2.100.000,-
  • Non-Presenter / Attendee: Rp 1.500.000,-

More information & submission guidelines:
🌐 https://icodse.org
📩 icodse@informatika.org

We look forward to receiving your contribution and welcoming you to Batam this October!

Warm regards,
2025 IEEE ICoDSE Organizing Committee

Juanda (Dago), close to many cafes and shops. Offers a relaxed environment with an indoor pool and a dedicated bar area. Provides spacious…
items.content_html:
Here are hotel recommendations, sorted by their proximity to the Institut Teknologi Bandung (ITB) campus.

Four Points by Sheraton Bandung

 
Star rating: 4.6 out of 5 stars 4.6
Category: 🏨 Hotel
Location : Click Here

Four Points by Sheraton Bandung is located approximately 1.4 km from the ITB main gate. It is a highly convenient choice for conference participants who want to be near in Dago street.

  • Features a refined atmosphere with modern rooms and suites.

  • Amenities include an outdoor pool with a bar, a fitness center, and on-site dining.

  • Located on the vibrant Jalan Ir. H. Juanda (Dago), close to many cafes and shops.

Novotel Bandung

 
Star rating: 4.6 out of 5 stars 4.6
Category: 🏨 Hotel
Location : Click Here
 
Novotel Bandung is roughly 2.1 km away from the campus. It sits in a very central area, making it easy for guests to navigate between the university and the Cihampelas shopping district.

  • Offers a relaxed environment with an indoor pool and a dedicated bar area.

  • Provides spacious suites suitable for business travelers.

  • Situated near the Cihampelas Walk (Ciwalk) mall for easy access to dining and entertainment.

Pullman Bandung Grand Central

 
Star rating: 4.9 out of 5 stars 4.9
Category: 🏨 Hotel
Location : Click Here
 
Pullman Bandung is located about 2.2 km from ITB. This is one of Bandung's newer luxury options, positioned right across from the iconic Gedung Sate.

  • Known for its upscale atmosphere, rooftop bar, and premium spa services.

  • Features an outdoor pool and a high-end fitness center.

  • Its location in the "Grand Central" area provides a prestigious backdrop for international guests.

Hilton Bandung

 
Star rating: 4.7 out of 5 stars 4.7
Category: 🏨 Hotel
Location : Click Here
 
Hiltong Bandung is approximately 3.1 km from the ITB campus. While a bit further south near the train station, it offers world-class luxury and is very convenient for those arriving by rail.

  • Highlights include a beautiful rooftop pool and a well-regarded day spa.

  • Offers a 24-hour gym and diverse dining options on-site.

  • Renowned for its large, modern rooms and consistent service quality.

Crowne Plaza Bandung

 
Star rating: 4.8 out of 5 stars 4.8
Category: 🏨 Hotel
Location : Click Here
 
Crowne Plaza Bandung is about 3.5 km away from ITB. It is situated in the historic Braga/Lembong area, perfect for guests who want to explore Bandung's heritage after the conference.

  • Features an infinity pool with expansive views of the city skyline.

  • Provides four different eateries, ranging from casual to fine dining.

  • Positioned in the heart of the business district with easy access to the historic Braga Street.

items.content_html:
Universitas Negeri Gorontalo (UNG)

Universitas Negeri Gorontalo (UNG) is located in the heart of Gorontalo, Indonesia. Known for its commitment to excellence in education, UNG offers a wide range of undergraduate and graduate programs across various disciplines, including education, science, engineering, and social sciences. The university is dedicated to fostering a dynamic academic environment that encourages innovation, critical thinking, and community engagement.

Explore Gorontalo: The Hidden Gem of Indonesia

Gorontalo, located on the northern coast of Sulawesi, is a province renowned for its stunning natural landscapes, rich cultural heritage, and warm hospitality. This hidden gem of Indonesia offers a unique blend of unspoiled beaches, lush forests, and historical sites, making it a perfect destination for travelers seeking both relaxation and adventure.

The city of Gorontalo serves as the provincial capital and is the cultural and economic hub of the region. Visitors can explore the city's charming colonial architecture, vibrant markets, and local cuisine, which is influenced by the diverse ethnic groups that call Gorontalo home. Whether you're strolling through the streets of Gorontalo or venturing out into the countryside, you're sure to be captivated by the beauty and tranquility of this region.

Swim with Whale Sharks at Botubarani

One of the most extraordinary experiences that Gorontalo offers is the opportunity to swim with whale sharks at Botubarani. Located just a short drive from the city, Botubarani is a small coastal village that has gained international recognition as a prime spot for whale shark encounters. These gentle giants, the largest fish in the ocean, can be seen up close in the clear, warm waters of Botubarani.

The whale shark swimming tour in Botubarani is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. Visitors can snorkel or dive alongside these magnificent creatures, observing their graceful movements as they glide through the water. The tour is carefully managed to ensure the safety of both the sharks and the participants, making it a responsible and sustainable tourism activity.

The village of Botubarani has embraced its role as a guardian of these incredible animals, and the local community plays an active part in conservation efforts. By participating in this tour, visitors not only get to experience the awe-inspiring presence of whale sharks but also contribute to the preservation of marine life in Gorontalo.

items.title: Registration Page
items.summary: Registration Method Registration link will be provided in this page after acceptance notification date. Online registration for 2026 IEEE ICoDSE will be open in 8 September 2026, and will be closed in 22 September 2026. Please prepare your student ID (if applicable), presentation video & slide (for online presenter), and proof of payment before proceeding. International Domestic *Student presenters should upload their valid student ID. Recent graduates are eligible by providing proof of graduation date.
items.content_html:
Registration Method

Online registration for 2026 IEEE ICoDSE will be open in 8 September 2026, and will be closed in 22 September 2026. Please prepare your student ID (if applicable), presentation video & slide (for online presenter), and proof of payment before proceeding. 

Registration Fee

International

Presenter USD 300
Presenter (IEEE) USD 250
Presenter (Student) USD 250
Presenter (Student - IEEE) USD 200
Non Presenter USD 150

Domestic

Presenter Rp 3.500.000,-
Presenter (IEEE) Rp 3.000.000,-
Presenter (Student) Rp 2.500.000,-
Presenter (Student - IEEE) Rp 2.100.000,-
Non Presenter Rp 1.500.000,-

Registration Method

*Student presenters should upload their valid student ID. Recent graduates are eligible by providing proof of graduation date.

Methods of Payment

  • The available payment method is by Wire Transfer.
  • The conference committee must receive the full amount of the registration fee. Delegates should bear the Service Charge (if any) of the bank in the Sender’s Side. Please write your paper number on the remark column of the bank slip/transfer receipt.
  • The registration fee covers publication and proceeding.
  • An acknowledgement of your registration will be emailed to you, immediately after completion of online registration.
  • A Registration Confirmation will be provided after clearance of the full payment. Please note that wire transfer usually takes few days for processing.

Parallel Session I

Session I-A – Data mining and data tools

Thursday, 7 September2023, 13:00-15:00 GMT+7, Room A

  1. Promotion Abuse Fraud Detection Application Development using Risk Scoring
    Shafira Naya Aprisadianti and Latifa Dwiyanti
  2. Migration From Relational Database to Column-Oriented NoSQL Database
    Rizky Anggita Syarbaini Siregar and Fazat Nur Azizah
  3. Structured Spatial Three-Dimensional Objects Data Extension in PostgreSQL
    Kevin Ryan and Tricya Widagdo
  4. SQL Interface For CRUD Operations On Cloud Firestore
    Farhan N. H. Denira and Fazat N. Azizah
  5. Utilizing PostGIS Extension to Process Spatial Data Stored in Neo4j Database
    Jon Felix Germinian and Tricya Esterina Widagdo

Session I-B – Software and education

Thursday, 7 September2023, 13:00-15:00 GMT+7, Room B

  1. Development of Simulator for Learning Scrum
    Irvin Andryan Pratomo and Yani Widyani
  2. Development of a Scrum Learning Model Using Gamification with the Battleship Board Game
    Christian Gunawan and Wikan Danar Sunindyo
  3. Predicting Students Performance Using Data Mining Approach (Case Study: IT Del)
    Jaime Christ Bonar Sirait, Parmonangan R. Togatorop, Paulus M.L.P Tambunan, Yemima Febe Yanti Marpaung, Samuel I.G Situmeang and Humasak T.A Simanjuntak
  4. Dispositions as an Important Component in Computing Curriculum Development in IT Del
    Arlinta Barus, Inggriani Liem, Arnaldo Sinaga, Eka Sinambela, Tahan Sihombing and Sari Silalahi
  5. Utilization of Virtual Reality for Docker Learning
    Muhammad Fikri. N and Fitra Arifiansyah

Session I-C – Software application development

Thursday, 7 September2023, 13:00-15:00 GMT+7, Room C

  1. Omnichannel System Development for Integration with Various Marketplace Using Event-Driven Architecture
    Jordan Daniel Joshua and Muhammad Zuhri Catur Candra
  2. Development of an End-to-End Form Data Capture Model for an Electronic Election Recapitulation System
    James Chandra, Riza Satria Perdana and Saiful Akbar
  3. Offline Data Sharing on Android Application
    Muhammad Dzaki Razaan Faza, Riza Satria Perdana and Saiful Akbar
  4. Analysis and The Preliminary Design for Backend Technology of Disaster Management Information System
    Riyanthi Angrainy Sianturi and Jerry Andrianto Pangaribuan
  5. Election Data Visualization in Virtual Reality Environment Using Oculus Quest 2
    Vania Alya Qonita and Latifa Dwiyanti

Parallel Session II

Session II-A – AI for software engineering and AI applications

Thursday, 7 September2023, 15:15-17:15 GMT+7, Room A

  1. Raw mill Engine Failure Detection from Transformer Gated Convolutional Unit Networks
    Berliana Putri Muliatama, Imam Mukhlash, Mohammad Iqbal, Nurul Hidayat and Masaomi Kimura
  2. Experiment on Utilizing GPT-3.5 Language Model to Generate DSL of Data Management for Web Application Development using Qore-Base Platform
    Akifa Ufairah and Yani Widyani
  3. Comparison Machine Learning Technique For Grade Classification of Exported Cavendish Banana
    Junanto Prihantoro, Dhika Anbiya, Galuh Prihantoro, Erwin Nashrullah, Zaid Cahya and Riza Riza
  4. Driving Digital Transformation: Leveraging Robotic Process Automation (RPA) to Enhance Business Process Efficiency and Reducing Manual Errors

Session II-B – Software fundamentals and experiments

Thursday, 7 September 2023, 15:15-17:15 GMT+7, Room B

    Aghnia Nadhira Aliya Putri, Fadrian Dwiki Maulanda, Zulfiyandi Zulfiyandi, Devi Adrian and Zulfikar Hakim
  5. Achieving High-Level Software Component Summarization via Hierarchical Chain-of-Thought Prompting and Static Code Analysis
    Satrio Adi Rukmono, Lina Ochoa and Michel Chaudron

Session II-C – Software UI/UX and testing

Thursday, 7 September2023, 15:15-17:15 GMT+7, Room C

  1. Website Functionality Testing Using the Automation Tool
    Dr. Arlinta Christy Barus and Aprialdy Sembiring
  2. Interaction Design of Indonesian Junior High School Academic Information System using User-Centered Design
    Jeanne D'Arc Amara Hanieka and Fitra Arifiansyah
  3. Interaction Design Improvement of Content Preferences, Report, and Homepage feature of Shopee Video Using User-Centered Design
    Chintya Wijaya and Adi Mulyanto
  4. Interaction Design for Books Recommendation System to Improve User Satisfaction at EPerpusdikbud
    Falia Aufa Najla, Dana Sulistyo Kusumo and Arfive Gandhi
  5. Improving Cultural Objects Portal Application Usability Using User Usability Evaluation
    Budi Susanto, Kristofan Feriadi, Restyandito Restyandito, Gloria Virginia and Umi Proboyekti

Parallel Session III

Session III-A – Data engineering and tools

Friday, 8 September2023, 09:15-11:15 GMT+7, Room A

  1. Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis Model with Local Sentiment Aggregation for Online Travel Reviews
    Renny Pradina Kusumawardani, Mochammad Farrel Arrizal Kusuma, Radityo Prasetianto Wibowo and Aris Tjahyanto
  2. The Semantic Overlay Architecture for Data Interoperability and Exchange
    Fajar J. Ekaputra, Christoph Fabianek, Gabriel Unterholzer and Eduard Gringinger
  3. The Study Of Data Modeling Methodologies For Column-Oriented Databases
    Raden Haryo Pandu Prakoso and Fazat Nur Azizah
  4. Migration of Relational Database to NoSQL Document-Oriented Database
    Wilson Tandya and Fazat Nur Azizah
  5. Development of SQL Interface for Spatial Data Processing in XML Databases
    Muhammad Bintang Pananjung and Tricya Esterina Widagdo

Session III-B  – Secure software and software engineering tools

Friday, 8 September2023, 09:15-11:15 GMT+7, Room B

  1. Detecting Command Injection and Cross-site Scripting Vulnerabilities Using Graph Representations
    Kevin Fernaldy and Yudistira Asnar
  2. Exploring Low Rate Dos Attack Detection Methods: A Bibliometric Analysis and Roadmap for Further Research
    Anteng Widodo, Adian Fatchur Rochim and Budi Warsito
  3. A Static IDE Plugin to detect Security Hotspot for Laravel Framework Based Web Application
    Muhammad Anis Al Hilmi, Raswa Raswa, Robi Robiyanto, Daniel Oranova Siahaan, Alifia Puspaningrum and Hernawati Susanti Samosir

Session III-C  – Domain-specific machine learning models

Friday, 8 September2023, 09:15-11:15 GMT+7, Room C

  1. Improved Detection and Interpretation of Multilingual Signboards in Natural Scene for Visually Impaired People
    Qamar Zaman, Shah Khusro and A Min Tjoa
  2. Automated Chest X-Ray Report Generator Using Multi-Model Deep Learning Approach
    Arief Purnama Muharram, Hollyana Puteri Haryono, Abassi Haji Juma, Ira Puspasari and Nugraha Priya Utama
  3. Comparison of Support Vector Regression and Artificial Neural Network Algorithm in Predicting the Number of Prospective Student Registrans
    Herimanto Pardede, Arie Satia Dharma, Hotmangasi Manurung and Agnes Bertua Nababan
  4. A Real-Time Multi-Disease Prediction framework using Deep Learning Techniques
    Venkata Ramana Attada, Lakshmana Rao Kalabarige and Srividya Kotagiri
  5. Detection of Tuberculosis Disease using Deep Learning Techniques
    Geetamma Tummalapalli, Jami Kousik, Rajasekhar Mungi, Murapaka Rajesh, Kalyani Dinesh and Kadiyam Rajalingeswara Rao

Special thanks to Pertamina for making this conference happen for the progress of the data and software engineering industry in Indonesia

items.content_html:
2024 IEEE International Conference on Data and Software Engineering (ICoDSE) 

“Data-Driven Innovation: Transforming Industries and Societies”

🏅 Best Papers

CodeView: A Tool for Software Visualization in Development View
Christopher Jeffrey, Aditya Prawira Nugroho, Satrio Adi Rukmono and Yani Widyani 

LLM2Vec Sentence Embeddings Analysis in Sentiment Classification
Matthew Martianus Henry, Nur Adhianti Heryanto, Mahmud Isnan, Dian Kurnianingrum, Chyntia Ika Ratnapuri and Bens Pardamean

===

Date
October 30-31, 2024

Venue
Damhil Hotel, Gorontalo, Indonesia

Program Schedule

Time in the program schedule is in Central Indonesia Time / Waktu Indonesia Tengah (WITA; GMT+8). Please pay attention and adjust it to your local time. Current time in WITA can be found on time.is/Gorontalo.

Day I – Wednesday, 30 October 2024

07.30–08.00

Registration

08.00–09.40

Opening + photo sessions

09.40–10.40

Keynote 1: Prof. David Lo, Ph.D. (Singapore Management University, Singapore)

10.40–11.40

Keynote 2: Prof. Taufiq Asyhari (Monash University, Indonesia)

11.40–11.45

Short Break

11.45–13.05

Parallel Session I  (4 rooms x 4 papers @20 minutes)

13.05–13.40

Lunch Break

13.40–15.20

Parallel Session II (4 rooms x 4 papers @20 minutes)

15.20–15.25

Short Break

15.25–16.10

Keynote 3: O. Univ. Prof. Dr. techn. A Min Tjoa (Vienna University of Technology, Austria)

16.10–16.15

Coffee Break

16.15–17.35

Parallel Session III (4 rooms x 4-5 papers @20 minutes)

17.35–18.20

Break

18.20–20.40

Dinner (with Meet and Greet)

Day II – Thursday, 31 October 2024

06.30–07.00

Registration

07.00–11.00

Social Event: (Sightseeing around Gorontalo)

Location: Whale Sharks at Botubarani, Gorontalo

11.00–12.30

Gala Lunch, Chairman Committee Report (Dr. Riza Satria Perdana, S.T., M.T.)
Best paper Awards Announcement, and Closing ceremony

Location :  Oceana Resto & Resort

Parallel Session I

Session I-A

  • Development of a Module for Information Extraction and Curriculum Vitae Classification for Candidate Matching Using Machine Learning Model
    Muhammad Risqi Firdaus, Agung Dewandaru and Gusti Ayu Putri Saptawati Soekidjo
  • Implementation of Exact Machine Unlearning Algorithm in Credit Scoring Cases
    Ilham Pratama and Windy Gambetta
  • Evolving Student Expectations in Information Systems: A Three-Year Trend Analysis
    Rahmat Taufik R L Bau, Hermila A and Nayla Putri Salsabila
  • (*)AI as Companion Interaction Design in Internal Developer Portals: A Path to Enhanced Developer Experience
    Yasya Rusyda Aslina and I Gusti Bagus Baskara Nugraha

Session I-B

  • Text Augmentation to Overcome Data Limitations in Sentiment Analysis
    Muhammad Nasry Ashar and Daniel Oranova Siahaan
  • Sentence Embedding-based Soft Clustering for Analyzing Texts of Student Outcomes
    Steven Siahaan and Masayu Khodra
  • Development of a Text-Based Anti-Cheat Component Using ALBERT for Detecting Cheating in Automated Interviews
    Yakobus Iryanto Prasethio, Agung Dewandaru and G A Putri Saptawati
  • (*)LLM Alignment with Prompt Engineering and RAG (Case Study: Conversational Agent for the "MIST" Diary System
    Zhillan Attarizal Rezyarifin and Riza Satria Perdana

Session I-C

  • A Secure Software Development Methodology for Enterprise Business Applications
    Hrishikesh Joshi
  • Enhanced Multi-SARIMA Model for Anomaly Detection in Multi-Seasonal Time Series Data
    Ashton Williams and Soon Chung
  • An Ontology for Capturing and Integrating Statistical Data Production Metadata
    Sulthoni Ashiddiiqi, Fajar J Ekaputra, Muhammad Zuhri Catur Candra and Gusti Ayu Putri Saptawati
  • (*)Microservice-Based Backend Architecture for Software-as-a-Service Queuing System
    Riandy Hasan and Riza Satria Perdana

Session I-D

  • Allergen Information Interaction Design in Food Delivery Application Using User-Centered Design Approach
    Natasha Tiovanny Silaban and Wikan Danar Sunindyo
  • Microinteractions Development for Enhancing Usability in Klik Indomaret Application
    Faizah Kamilah Setiawan and Adi Mulyanto
  • A Next Generation Web Browser Execution Environment
    Zahir Toufie and Boniface Kabaso
  • User-Centered Interaction Design for Indonesia's E-Government Passport Application: The M-Paspor Indonesia Case Study
    Claudia and Dessi P Lestari

Parallel Session II

Session II-A

  • LLM2Vec Sentence Embeddings Analysis in Sentiment Classification
    Matthew Martianus Henry, Nur Adhianti Heryanto, Mahmud Isnan, Dian Kurnianingrum, Chyntia Ika Ratnapuri and Bens Pardamean
  • Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) Application on Classification of Diseases and Symptoms of Pest Infestation on Plants (Case Study on Rice Plant Leaves)
    William Manuel Kurniawan, Windy Gambetta and Rika Alfianny
  • Classification of Hashtag's Popularity Growth Patterns Using Shape Dynamic Time Warping
    Ren Hayashi and Yasuhiro Hashimoto
  • (*)Go vs Java: A Detailed Performance Analysis in Concurrent Programming
    Md Neaz Morshed and Prince Roy

Session II-B

  • Utilisation of Artificial Intelligence through ChatGPT in Teaching: A Comprehensive Review of Perspectives and Future Directions
    Hermila A , Rahmat Taufik R L Bau and Mukhlisulfatih Latief
  • Development of LLM-Based System for IT Talent Interview
    David K H Pasaribu, Agung Dewandaru and G A Putri Saptawati
  • Optimizing White Blood Cell Nuclei Segmentation: A Proficient Method for Improved Diagnostic Accuracy
    Abdulaziz Ahmed Siyad, Abdisalam Abdulkadir Adam, Ibrahim Abdullahi Farah, Mohamed Abdirahman Yusuf and Bashir Abdirahman Hussein
  • Image Captioning on Diseased Chili Plants Using ResNeXt50 and Transformer
    Dwi Ratna Sulistyaningrum, Budi Setiyono and Muhammad Khusni Nailul Mubarok

Session II-C

  • An Initial Analysis of Newcomers based on Labels in Contemporary GitHub Projects
    Fadhlih Hasan Setiawan, Yusuf Sulistyo Nugroho and Raula Gaikovina Kula
  • Micro Frontend Application Shell and Module Federation Architecture implementation and comparison
    Marcos Roberto G Bahiense-Junior, Lanier M dos Santos, Renan R Bezerra and Erick C Bezerra
  • Development of Cascading Circuit Breaker System using Event-Driven Approach in Microservices
    Mohamad Daffa Argakoesoemah and Muhammad Zuhri Catur Candra
  • PERISAI: Design and Implementation of a Remote Deployment System for IoT Applications
    Muhammad Garebaldhie and Muhammad Zuhri Catur Candra

Session II-D

  • Assessment Tool for Software Failure and Risk Mitigation in Project Management
    Mohammad Ibraigheeth
  • Optimizing Load Forecasting in Smart Grids with AI-Driven Solutions
    Jamshaid Iqbal Janjua
  • ReduJavator: A Tool to Simplify Developer-written Java Unit Tests
    Brandon Wilber, Tuan Le and Arpit Christi 
  • Collaborative Virtual Reality for Short-Range Combat Training: System Design and Initial Technical Evaluation
    Melisa Rende and Kusno Prasetya

Parallel Session III

Session III-A

  • An Enhanced Vigenère Encryption Algorithm using 6S-Substitution and 2S-Transposition Technique
    Sugar Mae Galeza, Ariel Sison and Ruji Medina
  • A systematic literature review of the application of Agile applied to Large-scale, safety-critical, cyber-physical systems
    Robin Yeman and Yashwant Malaiya
  • Analysis of In-Processing Algorithms Meeting Monotonic Selective Risk Conditions for Fairness and Accuracy in Machine Learning
    Nayotama Pradipta and Windy Gambetta
  • Detection of Broken Access Control Vulnerability Using Graph Representation
    Fawwaz Anugrah Wiradhika Dharmasatya and Yudistira Asnar

Session III-B

  • On Performance Comparison Between Strong Machine Unlearning Algorithms for Logistic Regression Credit Assessment Models
    Jeremy Syaloom Okey Nathanael Simbolon and Windy Gambetta
  • Curriculum Vitae Matching Module Development Using Semantic Textual Similarity in the Curriculum Vitae Screening Application
    Kevin Roni, Gusti Ayu Putri Saptawati, Ricardo Irwan Rei, Yoseph Alexander Siregar and Rizky Akbar Asmaran
  • Development of Machine Learning Module in Intrusion Detection System for Unknown Threat Detection
    Christine Hutabarat and Yudistira Asnar
  • (*)An Authenticated Framework for Supply Chain Management
    Prince Roy, Rajneesh Kumar and Md Neaz Morshed

Session III-C

  • Application of Semi-Supervised Learning for Job Applicant Competency Classification
    Steven Wen and Putri Soekidjo
  • Enterprise Architecture Practical in Telecommunication Industry
    Muhammad Rayhan and Muhammad Mahreza Maulana
  • Blockchain-based Public Key Infrastructure using Smart Contracts
    Frederik Louis and Muhammad Zuhri Catur Candra
  • The Development of Blockchain-Based Access Control with a Decentralized XACML Approach: A Case Study on Payment Gateway
    I Gede Arya Raditya Parameswara and Muhammad Zuhri Catur Candra
  • (*)Measuring the Impact of Technical Debt on Development Effort in Software Projects
    Kartik Gupta

Session III-D

  • CodeView: A Tool for Software Visualization in Development View
    Christopher Jeffrey, Aditya Prawira Nugroho, Satrio Adi Rukmono and Yani Widyani
  • SRS for Software with Machine Learning Features
    Muhammad Helmi Hibatullah and Yani Widyani
  • Use Case Generator Based on User Stories
    Hana Fathiyah and Yani Widyani
  • (*)Optimal Cloud Infrastructure for Software-as-a-Service Queuing System
    Muhammad Rey Shazni Helmi and Riza Satria Perdana

Zoom conference link will be sent via email, for those who have registered.
(*): To be confirmed

Social Events

Notes for the social events:
- They are two options to watch the whale sharks: watching from the boat, or swimming with them in the sea (yes, they are big, but friendly).
- Please do not touch the whale sharks, because they have sensitive skins.
- Snorkeling gears, fins, and life vests are available if you need them.
- Changing rooms and bathrooms are also available.
